comments.body: Just tried skopeo to copy from a docker registry to local dir and yeah, it seems to download each layer sequentially and it's pretty slow.

It'd also be awesome if existing layers would be skipped, at the moment I'm creating about 90 layers for an image and all but 2 could be skipped, yet it uploaded every single one again, which took forever.

Are there any alternatives for copying image tarballs to a registry without a docker daemon? Been searching a lot but skopeo is the only one "working" right now.

> I found this patch to parallelize the upload: agonzalezro/image@8d49c78

I think there’s a substantially similar PR around here somewhere — but the transports just can’t work safely when naively making several **EDIT <s>transports</s>transfers** in parallel like that.  Truly reworking this to be parallel would be a much larger work.

> It'd also be awesome if existing layers would be skipped, at the moment I'm creating about 90 layers for an image and all but 2 could be skipped, yet it uploaded every single one again, which took forever.

I’m working on that now, but it may take a few more days/weeks.

After some more searching, I finally found https://github.com/google/go-containerregistry/blob/master/cmd/crane/doc/crane.md which supports skipping layers but is still sequential as far as I can tell.

@manveru I am not using the patch I created, it was just a POC to show to @runcom but after talking with him he has shown me that, as @mtrmac said, there is much more work than just parallelizing the download.

If I can help somehow I would be happy to do it.
I believe we have support for this now.  @vrothberg reopen if I am incorrect.

Hi there, I cannot seem to make this feature work. Is there some sort of flag or something? I am on 0.1.37 by the way. Here is an example command that I use:

```
skopeo copy docker://xxx-docker-virtual.artifactory-yyy.zzz.com/qqq/www:1.7.0 docker-archive:/home/jenkins/agent/workspace/xxx/SOURCES/xxx/images/www-1.7.0.tar:www:1.7.0
```
It would be really nice if you can help me out! Thanks!

Hi @erenatas, thanks for reaching out. For parallel copying, the source and the destination transports must both support it. In your case, the `docker-archive` transport doesn't support it. When you copy into container-storage or to another registry, the blobs will be copied in parallel.
@rhatdan @mtrmac, maybe we should document which transports support that but I wonder where to put it best? `containers-transports`?
Yes I think we should document it in container-transports.

@vrothberg Still new to all this so please bear with me.  So your saying that if you copy from docker-archive to a remote, it will be slow because docker-archive doesn't support parallel copy.

1) In my use case, I use kaniko to generate a docker image and then copy from local (docker-archive:// to docker://).  Does this mean that in my case there is no way to really improve performance with the parallel copy?
2) containers-transports was mentioned earlier stating that it supports it.  Since i'm using kaniko there is probably no way I can take advantage of that since there is no container storage locally.

```
  containers-storage:docker-reference An image located in a local containers/storage image store. Location and image store specified in /etc/containers/storage.conf
```
Any advice appreciated, 
Thanks,
Derek

>  So your saying that if you copy from docker-archive to a remote, it will be slow because docker-archive doesn't support parallel copy.

@darewreck54, exactly, yes.

However, I believe that with a bit massaging of the code, we can make docker-archive parallel (i.e., after extracting the archive upload the layers in parallel).

Can kaniko push to a registry? If so, you could run a local registry and then use Skopeo to push from there.

Kaniko I believe only create images. I think docker supports rootless now but not sure if it allows for local registry. I’m basically doing this all from a kubernetes  container which I don’t have docker installed

When you say massaging the code, are you referring to skopeo code? From following the github issue thread it didn’t seem like that was an option? Do you know the feature is being worked on?

Thanks
Derek

@darewreck54, apologies. After more coffees I noticed that copying from `docker-archive` to `docker` (i.e., a registry) _does_ work in parallel.

> @darewreck54, apologies. After more coffees I noticed that copying from `docker-archive` to `docker` (i.e., a registry) _does_ work in parallel.

Do you mean it doesn't or does?

> Hi @erenatas, thanks for reaching out. For parallel copying, the source and the destination transports must both support it. In your case, the `docker-archive` transport doesn't support it. When you copy into container-storage or to another registry, the blobs will be copied in parallel.

Has something changed since this thread?

The behavior of sources and destinations is independent. In the case of docker-archive, it supports parallel operation as a _source_ (after uncompressing the archive, parts of it can be read in parallel — as long as the destination supports parallel operation as well), but it does not support parallel operation as a _destination_ (creating a sequential file is necessarily sequential, unless we wanted to gamble on creating separate large on-disk copies first, in parallel (if they even fit!) and creating a sequential archive later, and hoping that this somehow ends up being faster).

The earlier question was about a destination; you are asking about a source.
